content: Post-cardiac arrest care: optimize hemodynamics (MAP ≥65 mmHg, often higher needed for cerebral perfusion), oxygenation (SpO2 92-98%, avoid hyperoxia), ventilation (normocapnia, PaCO2 35-45 mmHg), glucose control (140-180 mg/dL). Targeted Temperature Management (TTM): TTM2 trial (2021) showed no difference between 33°C vs strict normothermia with fever prevention in comatose OHCA patients; current guidelines recommend active temperature control with fever prevention (≤37.5°C) for at least 72 hours, target 32-36°C reasonable. Coronary angiography: immediate for STEMI or suspected cardiac cause; delayed/selective for others. Neurologic prognostication: wait ≥72 hours after return to normothermia, use multimodal approach (clinical exam, EEG, SSEP, MRI, biomarkers NSE/S100B). Avoid premature withdrawal of life support. Seizure prophylaxis/treatment common (up to 20% have seizures). Consider ECMO-CPR (extracorporeal CPR) for refractory arrest in specialized centers—EOLIA and other trials show benefit in selected patients. Rehabilitation and psychological support for survivors.
